Title: User Registration
Preconditions: The Android app is installed on the device.
Test Steps:
1. Launch the app.
2. Click on the option to create an account.
3. Enter a username, password, and email in the registration form.
4. Submit the form.
Expected Result:
Notification of successful registration.
User redirected to the Home page.
User data and device used for login saved in the database.

Title: User Login
Preconditions: The user has installed the app and created an account.
Test Steps:
1. Launch the app.
2. Click on the login option.
3. Enter the username and password in the login form.
4. Submit the form.
Expected Result:
User authenticated and redirected to the main application page.
Device used for login saved in the database.

Title: View Profile
Test Steps: 1. Click on the profile view button.
Expected Result:
Display of user's account information (username and email).
Option to modify profile or password.

Title: Edit Profile
Preconditions: User is on the profile view page.
Test Steps:
1. Click on the edit profile button.
2. Modify the user's account information (e.g., email or password).
3. Save the changes.
Expected Result:
Updated profile data stored in the database.
User redirected to the profile view page.

Title: Delete Profile
Preconditions: User is on the profile view page.
Test Steps:
1. Click on the delete profile button.
2. Confirm the deletion.
Expected Result:
User profile and related data removed from the database.
User redirected to the login page.

Title: Logout
Preconditions: User is on the profile view page.
Test Steps: 1. Click on the logout button.
Expected Result:
User logged out.
Redirected to the login page.

Title: View Carbon Footprint
Test Steps: 1. Click on the carbon footprint scan button.
Expected Result: Display of the user's carbon footprint.

Title: Hardware Scan
Preconditions: User is on the hardware scan page.
Test Steps: 1. Click on the scan button.
Expected Result: Display of hardware status and health.

Title: Change Device Components
Test Steps:
1. Click on the change components button.
2. Complete the quick questionnaire.
3. Confirm or cancel the changes.
Expected Result:
Database updated with the correct carbon footprint calculation.

Title: User must accept terms of use upon first login
Preconditions: User has not logged into the application before
Test Steps:
1. Launch the application
2. Attempt to log in for the first time
3. Verify that the application prompts the user to read and accept the terms of use
Expected Result: The application displays a screen with the terms of use and requires the user to accept them before proceeding.

Title: User can create a profile
Preconditions: User has accepted the terms of use
Test Steps:
1. Log in to the application
2. Navigate to the profile creation section
3. Fill out the required fields in the profile creation form
4. Submit the form
Expected Result: The application creates a new user profile and stores the entered data.

Title: User can update their profile
Test Steps:
1. Log in to the application
2. Navigate to the profile settings
3. Modify one or more fields in the profile
4. Save the changes
Expected Result: The application updates the user's profile with the new information.

Title: User can log out of the application
Preconditions: User is logged into the application
Test Steps:
1. Log in to the application
2. Navigate to the logout option
3. Confirm the logout action
Expected Result: The application logs the user out and clears the session.

Title: User can cancel their profile
Test Steps:
1. Log in to the application
2. Navigate to the profile cancellation option
3. Confirm the cancellation action
Expected Result: The application deletes the user's profile and removes the associated data from the database.

Title: Application displays predicted carbon footprint
Test Steps:
1. Log in to the application
2. Navigate to the carbon footprint calculation section
3. Select the device or component to calculate the impact
4. Review the displayed carbon footprint
Expected Result: The application shows the predicted carbon footprint based on the selected device or component.

Title: Application removes its data upon uninstallation
Test Steps:
1. Install the application
2. Uninstall the application
3. Check the device storage for remaining application files
Expected Result: The application removes all its data and files from the device storage.

Title: Application deletes user's personal data
Test Steps:
1. Log in to the application
2. Navigate to the data deletion option
3. Confirm the deletion action
Expected Result: The application deletes the user's personal data and related sensitive information from the database.

Title: Application scans hardware details
Preconditions: User is logged into the application
Test Steps:
1. Log in to the application
2. Navigate to the hardware scan section
3. Scan the device
4. Review the scanned hardware details
Expected Result: The application displays the hardware details including memory, battery status, and device model. <|end|>

Title: Application Response Time
Test Steps:
1. Perform a data retrieval operation.
2. Measure the response time.
3. Repeat the operation multiple times to ensure consistency.
Expected Result: The response time should be under 100 milliseconds.

Title: User Interface Simplicity
Test Steps:
1. Attempt to navigate through the application.
2. Check if the user interface is intuitive and easy to use.
3. Verify that there are no overlapping or too small selection options.
Expected Result: The user interface should be simple and intuitive.

Title: Database Protection from SQL Injection
Test Steps:
1. Attempt to inject SQL code into the application.
2. Verify that the application prevents SQL injection attacks.
Expected Result: The application should prevent SQL injection attacks and protect sensitive data.

Title: Design Pattern Compliance
Test Steps:
1. Review the source code of the application.
2. Confirm that the MVC design pattern is followed.
Expected Result: The application should adhere to the MVC design pattern.

Title: Deliverable Timeliness
Test Steps:
1. Check the delivery status of the project milestones.
2. Ensure that all deliverables are submitted according to the course schedule.
Expected Result: All deliverables should be submitted on time.

Title: Firebase Usage
Test Steps: 1. Verify that Firebase is used as the database management system.
Expected Result: Firebase should be the chosen database management system.

Title: Data Handling Compliance
Test Steps:
1. Review the application's data handling practices.
2. Ensure that the application complies with European and Italian guidelines.
Expected Result: The application should comply with relevant data handling guidelines.

Title: Internet Connectivity Requirement
Test Steps:
1. Check if the application requires an internet connection.
2. Verify that the application can communicate with the database over the internet.
Expected Result: The application should require an internet connection for proper functionality.

Title: Ethical Data Usage
Test Steps:
1. Review the application’s data usage policies.
2. Ensure that the data is not used for profit.
Expected Result: The application should not use the calculated data for commercial purposes.

Title: Simple Actions Execution Time
Test Steps:
1. Change a screen in the application.
2. Measure the execution time.
Expected Result: The execution time for simple actions should be less than 100 milliseconds.

Title: Intuitive User Interface
Test Steps:
1. Navigate through the application.
2. Assess the intuitiveness of the user interface.
Expected Result: The user interface should be intuitive.

Title: Database Protection from XSS Attacks
Test Steps:
1. Attempt to inject malicious scripts into the application.
2. Verify that the application prevents XSS attacks.
Expected Result: The application should prevent XSS attacks and protect sensitive data.

Title: Design Pattern Usage
Test Steps:
1. Review the source code of the application.
2. Confirm that a design pattern is used for development.
Expected Result: The application should use a design pattern for development.

Title: Internet Connection for Data Modification
Test Steps:
1. Modify and control information within the application.
2. Verify that an internet connection is required.
Expected Result: The application should require an internet connection for modifying and controlling information. <|end|>

Title: User is informed about resources and privacy

Test Steps:
1. Launch the application on the device.
2. Verify that the user is informed about the resources the application will use and about privacy.
Expected Result: The user receives information about the resources used and privacy policies.

Test Steps:

2. Follow the registration process.
